residualRisks: MockupPreviews is still a large composition file, although the repeated spec and gallery-control structures have now been extracted into exported shared surfaces.
Storybook build still emits upstream chunk-size and eval warnings from Storybook tooling, but the build completes successfully.
implementation.summary: Extracted shared mockup gallery controls and spec-list primitives out of page-local markup, moved the example app onto the shared controls surface, expanded the code/editor/diff family for metadata-heavy and one-sided states, added direct Storybook coverage for the new preview and code variants, and hardened key dark-mode and focus/contrast states across dense chat/docs/mock surfaces.

focusAreas.label: Preview controls extraction and consumer parity
focusAreas.reason: The example app currently owns preview control composition that should be sharable and story-consumable as an exported mockup-gallery surface.

focusAreas.label: Page-local mock structure extraction
focusAreas.reason: Several visually important structures are still implicit page composition plus CSS convention instead of reusable primitives or explicit variants.

focusAreas.label: Code/editor/diff family maturity
focusAreas.reason: The current APIs are too shallow for richer metadata, alternate framing, embedded docs/chat usage, and one-sided or metadata-heavy diff states.

focusAreas.label: Dark mode and accessibility hardening
focusAreas.reason: Interactive states, dense metadata, chips, tabs, and dark surfaces still need contrast and focus-state strengthening with inspection coverage.

focusAreas.label: Storybook completion
focusAreas.reason: Important extracted motifs and state variants are not all directly inspectable today, especially for dark mode and state-sensitive surfaces.

implementationSteps: Extract a shared mockup-gallery control surface from the example app into src/mockups so Storybook and the example both exercise the same exported controls and labels.
Componentize the remaining page-local structures discovered in MockupPreviews by adding focused exported primitives or explicit variants instead of composition-only CSS tricks.

Expand the code/editor/diff APIs for richer headers, metadata rows, framing variants, empty and one-sided states, and better embedding across docs/chat/mock surfaces; update consumers to use the richer shared surface rather than ad hoc wrappers.

Harden dark-mode and accessibility styling in shared CSS for tabs, buttons, chips, metadata, docs margins, chat rails, tool cards, inspector panels, and code surfaces, including focus-visible and selected states.
Add Storybook stories for the new or expanded shared motifs and for dark-mode or accessibility-sensitive states so each important variant is directly inspectable.
Update the example app to consume only exported package surfaces and shared preview controls, then remove local duplication that became package-level API.
risks: MockupPreviews is large, so extraction work must stay targeted to structures that clearly repeat or are required by the example/story surfaces.
The code/diff API expansion touches shared CSS and multiple stories, so there is moderate regression risk around layout density and dark-theme contrast.
Storybook and example parity changes may reveal missing exports or package-entry assumptions that the current demo shell was hiding.

runtimeCallPaths.reason: The package entry point defines the public export surface and the CSS loaded by downstream consumers; any reusable primitive or API change must remain coherent here.
The React-only consumer entry point is what the example app uses for shared surfaces, so parity and ergonomics issues show up here first.
MockupPreviews is still the densest composition surface and currently holds several page-local structures that should become reusable exported primitives or clearer variants.
The code/editor/diff family is reused by docs, chat, and preview surfaces; API and theme-depth changes need to land here and then be exercised by stories and mockups.
The example app is the real package-consumer validation surface and must stop carrying package-worthy preview UI logic locally.
Storybook is the primary inspection surface for reusable kit maturity, so direct stories and dark-mode renderings need to cover the extracted motifs and interaction states.

build.summary: Package build passed and emitted dist bundles plus declarations.

exampleBuild.summary: Example app build passed after installing its local file-based package dependency, validating the shared exported consumer surface.

lint.summary: TypeScript lint gate passed after installing package dependencies in the worktree.

storybook.summary: Storybook build passed with the new preview-primitives and expanded code stories. Remaining warnings were upstream Storybook eval/chunk-size warnings only.

tests.summary: Vitest passed: 2 test files, 7 tests, 0 failures.

instructions: Summarize the major reusable-surface improvements, API changes, story/example parity changes, and dark-mode or accessibility hardening.
List only material residual risks or follow-up opportunities.
prompt.outputFormat:
JSON with:
  summary: string,
  validationsPassed: string[],
  residualRisks: string[]
prompt.role: principal reviewer summarizing a design-system maturation pass
prompt.task: Review the completed work and produce a concise final outcome summary.

description: Summarize the implemented changes, validated gates, and any remaining risk.
title: Summarize maturation outcome
